disclaimer: NyayaLens provides legal information for education and issue spotting only. It is not legal advice and does not create a lawyer-client relationship.
demo_corpus_notice: The local corpus may contain simplified demo sources. Replace them with official legal sources before real-world use.

issue_detected.refusal_message: I cannot help with threats, forged evidence, impersonation, harassment, illegal lock-breaking, or other unlawful pressure tactics. A safer path is to preserve evidence, communicate in writing, ask for itemized reasons, and consult legal aid or a qualified lawyer for high-risk steps.

safety_result.reason: User intent asks for unlawful pressure, fabrication, impersonation, or harassment.

expert_route.route_reason: Request needs safety handling and legal-aid oriented guidance.

warnings: State is missing or uncertain; state-specific law may apply.

citation.title: Legal Aid and Safety General Information
citation.excerpt:
DEMO CORPUS: This is a simplified educational placeholder. Replace with official legal sources before real-world use.

Legal Aid and Safety General Information

For high-risk employment or tenancy disputes, consider speaking with a qualified lawyer, legal aid clinic, district legal services authority, labour department helpdesk, or appropriate local rent/civil authority depending on the state and facts.

Do not forge evidence, impersonate officials or lawyers, threaten the other party, blackmail, break locks, or publish private information. Use written, polite, factual communication and kee...

citation.title: Tenancy General Information
citation.excerpt: Rent increase, lock-in period, repairs, and maintenance disputes depend on the agreement, local law, notices, and evidence of condition or payment.

citation.title: Tenancy General Information
citation.excerpt:
DEMO CORPUS: This is a simplified educational placeholder. Replace with official legal sources before real-world use.

Tenancy General Information

Tenants and landlords should preserve the rent agreement, rent receipts, deposit proof, move-in and move-out photos, inventory lists, repair bills, messages, and written notices.

Security deposit deductions should be explained with an itemized basis such as unpaid rent, documented damage, agreed painting charges, or repair invoices. Where the agreement is unclear or no itemized bill is provided, the dispute needs careful evidence review.

Evict...

citation.title: Contract Clause General Information
citation.excerpt:
DEMO CORPUS: This is a simplified educational placeholder. Replace with official legal sources before real-world use.

Contract Clause General Information

Arbitration clauses may affect the forum or process for dispute resolution. Jurisdiction clauses may identify a city or court, but their effect depends on the contract and applicable law.

For deductions, recovery, or withholding clauses, ask for the written clause, itemized calculation, proof of loss or expense, and the legal basis for deduction. Avoid verbal-only communication and preserve copies of all notices and replies.

This educa...

citation.title: Employment Exit General Information
citation.excerpt:
Post-employment non-compete restrictions need careful legal review. Broad restrictions after employment may be difficult to enforce, but confidentiality and non-solicitation obligations can still matter depending on facts and contract wording.

Long notice periods should be checked against the signed contract, appointment letter, role category, company policy, and applicable state-specific employment law. If jurisdiction is unclear, confidence should be reduced.

potential_provision_matches.source_quote:
THE BHARATIYA NYAYA SANHITA, 2023
NO. 45 OF 2023
[25th December, 2023.]
An Act to consolidate and amend the provisions relating to offences and for
matters connected therewithor incidentalthereto.
BE it enacted by Parliament in the Seventy-fourth Year of the Republic of India as
follows:––
CHAPTERI
PRELIMINARY
1. (1) ThisAct maybe called the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.
(2) It shall come intoforce on such date as the Central Government may, bynotification
in the Official Gazette, appoint, and different dates maybe appointed for different provisions
of this Sanhita.
Short title,
commencement
potential_provision_matches.why_relevant: Potentially relevant only because user text contains fraud, forgery, threat, blackmail, or similar allegation facts.
matched_facts: user text contains criminal-screening fact pattern: Help me forge evidence and threaten the landlord.

uncertainties: Only demo corpus material may have been retrieved; it is not a complete statement of Indian law.
State is missing or uncertain; state-specific law may apply.

steps: Do not use threats, forged evidence, impersonation, or confrontation.
Preserve the original documents and communication records.
Use a short written clarification request and seek legal aid for urgent risks.
Confirm the state/city and contract jurisdiction before choosing a filing or escalation route.
remedy_plan.draft_message: I want to resolve this through written communication. Please share the facts, documents, and calculation you are relying on so I can respond appropriately.
remedy_plan.escalation_note: For immediate safety concerns, contact local authorities or qualified legal aid.
